description: jQuery versions before 3.5.0 are affected by a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability: passing HTML from untrusted sources to DOM manipulation methods like .html() or .append() can execute untrusted code, even after the input was sanitized.
jQuery versions before 3.0.0 are affected by a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in jQuery.ajax() when using cross-domain requests, allowing execution of attacker-supplied JavaScript.

Bootstrap versions before 4.3.1 are affected by a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the tooltip and popover components, where the 'container' data attribute is not properly sanitized, allowing attacker-controlled HTML to be injected.
Bootstrap versions before 3.4.0 are affected by a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the 'data-target' attribute (affecting tooltip/popover/scrollspy), allowing arbitrary HTML/JS injection.

AngularJS (1.x) versions before 1.8.0 are affected by a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability where crafted 'ng-srcset' and similar attribute bindings can bypass Angular's built-in sanitization, allowing arbitrary script execution.

AngularJS (1.x) versions before 1.6.9 are affected by a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the '$sanitize' service, where certain crafted HTML can bypass sanitization and execute attacker-controlled scripts.

Lodash versions before 4.17.21 are affected by a command injection vulnerability via the 'template' function, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript if attacker-controlled input reaches the templating function.

Lodash versions before 4.17.19 are affected by a prototype pollution vulnerability in the 'zipObjectDeep' function, allowing attackers to modify JavaScript object prototypes and potentially achieve denial of service or remote code execution.

Moment.js versions before 2.29.4 are affected by a regular expression denial-of-service (ReDoS) vulnerability when parsing certain crafted date strings, allowing an attacker to degrade or hang the application.

Moment.js versions before 2.19.3 are affected by a regular expression denial-of-service (ReDoS) vulnerability that can be triggered through malicious user-supplied date strings, causing excessive CPU consumption.
